fact: January 17 is exactly 17 days into the new year - what are the odds of that perfect match?
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is typically prime time for ice fishing because lakes and ponds often have their thickest, strongest ice layers right now!
Capricorn season is almost over! People born on January 17 are some of the last Capricorns before Aquarius takes over on January 20.
Down in Antarctica, January 17 brings the warmest summer weather with nearly 24 hours of blazing sunlight every single day.
Tree sap runs slowest on days like this in cold climates - maple trees are basically taking a winter nap with their sugary blood moving like thick honey.
Arctic foxes are typically at their fluffiest right now, with winter coats so thick they look like walking cotton balls with tiny black noses.
The sun usually sets before 5 PM in most northern cities around January 17, often painting the sky in crazy orange and pink colors because of winter atmospheric conditions.
January 17 falls during the coldest stretch of winter in many northern places - meteorologists call this period the 'dead of winter' for a reason!
Penguins in the Southern Hemisphere are typically busy feeding their fluffy gray chicks right now during their peak summer breeding season.
Snow crystals often form their most perfect six-sided shapes when temperatures hit the sweet spot that happens around mid-January in snowy regions.
Bears in hibernation have usually been asleep for about two months by January 17, and their heart rate has slowed down to just 8 beats per minute!
This date sits almost exactly one month away from both the winter solstice behind it and the halfway point to spring ahead of it.
